title: Achyroseris bracteosa
taxonomy: Achyroseris bracteosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a member of the subfamily Cichorioideae, which includes plants with a similar morphology and reproductive structure. Within the subfamily, it belongs to the tribe Cichorieae, a group of plants characterized by their small to medium-sized flowers and often prickly leaves. The plant's classification is further supported by molecular studies, which have confirmed its placement within the Asteraceae family. Achyroseris bracteosa is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.

intro: Achyroseris bracteosa, also known as yareta or Chilean yareta, is a type of plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family, specifically a shrub-like species native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Achyroseris bracteosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Achyroseris bracteosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and capsules, making it a popular ingredient in traditional remedies and modern supplements.
